sanskrit:
यदहङ्कारमाश्रित्य न योत्स्य इति मन्यसे।
मिथ्यैष व्यवसायस्ते प्रकृतिस्त्वां नियोक्ष्यति॥
transliteration: yad ahaṅkāram āśritya na yotsya iti manyase | mithyaiṣa vyavasāyas te prakṛtis tvāṃ niyokṣyati ||
literal_meaning: If, relying on ego, you think 'I will not fight,' this resolve of yours is false; your own nature will compel you.
contextual_meaning: The psyche cannot indefinitely suppress its core traits; what is authentically 'you' will eventually override conscious resistance.
psychological_state: Ego-dystonic conflict: moral self-image clashes with warrior identity, producing intrusive violent impulses.
trigger_scenario: A pacifist-born soldier ordered into combat; the more they suppress the fight reflex, the stronger the intrusive violent dreams and startle responses become.
anchor_text: Your true nature will always win over forced denial.
